L1.area: Administrative backend panel and its data management modules

L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled filename is persistently stored and later rendered in administrative management views without proper output encoding. When a privileged user views the management panel, the malicious payload executes in their browser context, enabling privilege escalation and full account takeover across all roles.

L3.area: Backup module controller and its data listing and management response handling
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where a backup filename containing a malicious JavaScript payload is injected server-side via a crafted uploaded SQL file and stored without sanitization. The filename is later rendered without HTML entity encoding in the backup management panel's data table AJAX responses and deletion confirmation messages. When an administrator views or manages backups, the payload executes in their browser, enabling privilege escalation and full account takeover.

ground_truth.reason: The backup controller renders user-controlled backup filenames in AJAX data table responses and deletion messages without HTML escaping, allowing stored XSS when an administrator views the backup management panel.
